Marked Parcels | 2021

2021 Cabernet Sauvignon Beauregard Ranch

Beauregard Vineyards was founded in 1945 by Amos and Cecilia Beauregard. Five generations later, the vines now span 60 acres in Bonny Doon California.

Scores & Accolades

96 Points - Vinous

92 Points - Wine Enthusiast

Wine Specs

Estate detail with mountain precision

Beauregard Ranch Vineyard

Acres: 10
Vine age: 2005
Elevation: 1,740 to 1,802 feet

Site

Soil: Zayante Sand
Bedrock: Sand stone
Distance to Pacific Ocean: 4.56 miles

Ingredients

Estate grown Cabernet Sauvignon grapes, indigenous yeast, naturally occurring malolactic bacteria, minimal Sulphur Dioxide. Aged in French Oak Barrels.

Winemaker notes

Mountain Structure Meets Coastal Elegance

The Beauregard Ranch sits at an elevation where the vines are forced to struggle through ancient marine soils, battered by the cooling influence of the Pacific Ocean just miles away. This is mountain viticulture. The 2021 vintage delivered a wine of profound structural integrity. It is Cabernet Sauvignon with a backbone, built on a framework of firm tannins and searing, brilliant acidity.

  • In the Glass: Dark, impenetrable garnet.
  • The Nose: Crushed graphite, damp redwood needles, wild mountain mint, cassis, and dark cherry.
  • The Palate: Lean and muscular, with blackcurrant, dark plum, cedar box, tobacco leaf, espresso, fine-grained tannins, and an electric spine of acidity.

Grown, Produced & Bottled by Beauregard Vineyards, Santa Cruz, CA

The page concept is built to feel estate-driven and restrained, with the vineyard facts, critic scores, and structural tasting notes doing the persuasion work.
